title: SPLIT Lilith instead of Adam Personal thoughts debate
display_title: SPLIT Lilith instead of Adam Personal thoughts debate
wikitext:
[]: [SPLIT] Lilith instead of Adam: Personal thoughts/debate.             Quote Post by Lucretius » Thu Jun 17, 2010 3:52 pm Split from this thread . ~ SSD ________ There's still absolutely no reason for the identity of Lilith to be a production secret, from episode 15 onwards or otherwise. It's a detail that no one would care about except egf posters, certainly nowhere near as significant as Rei's being an artificial human (which is explicitly mentioned in the Proposal).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jun 17, 2010 4:11 pm Lucretius wrote: There's still absolutely no reason for the identity of Lilith to be a production secret,

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jun 17, 2010 4:22 pm Because there's nothing even remotely shocking about it to anyone who isn't Kaworu or an EGF veteran? This isn't anything like the reveal of Darth Vader being Luke Skywalker's father; that's something for which the consequences and importance are immediately apparent. This is just a big monster you know barely anything about actually having a different name that means equally little.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Jun 17, 2010 4:34 pm I have the impression that Lilith was the unplanned child, as if they suddenly decided in #24 that they couldn't make the giant on the cross be Adam (which up to that point was, according to Kaji).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jun 17, 2010 4:43 pm Essel wrote: Because there's nothing even remotely shocking about it to anyone who isn't Kaworu or an EGF veteran?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:10 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: You're telling me Japanese and American audiences who watched NGE on TV or DVD for the first time weren't shocked and confused when what they thought was "Adam" was revealed to be something entirely different called "Lilith"?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:13 pm Well seeing as we have no way of testing either theory except getting a complete NGE virgin to watch episodes 1-24, I guess you guys are going to have to agree to disagree about what shocks the audience.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:15 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:20 pm EDIT: This post was made before it was clear who Seele was responding to. My point is merely that essel says "It ISN'T noteworthy," and SSD says "It IS noteworthy," but that without evidence they're just going to go back and forth for awhile. One might argue that they already have gone back and forth a bit, and that unless something new is going to be brought to the table (the recollections of YOUR reaction, or someone else's, or evidence of a different sort), there ISN'T anything more to say on the subject of "When did Lilith become Lilith?"

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:24 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:25 pm Good to know. I'd contribute my recollections, but the first thing I found out about Evangelion was what each of the Angels were, so I never really got a chance to be "spoiled".

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:31 pm Legendary wrote: (the recollections of YOUR reaction, or someone else's, or evidence of a different sort)

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:34 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:41 pm I wasn't surprised when Adam turned out to be Lilith. I wasn't expecting it, but I certainly wasn't surprised. It was just like, "oh hey, that's neat." Then again, when I was watching it for the first time, I wasn't really thinking too deeply about it ("Are you now?", "No.").

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:52 pm I can vividly remember my experience with that reveal. Within the context of the episode, it was absolutely the linchpin. (Most of what I'm about to say I already said in the ep24' plothole/fanwank thread, but I think a fresh restatement fits.) When I first watched that episode, the perception I got of Kaworu was that he was a being to whom deception was alien. Right off the bat, he talked about "Lilin" with reckless abandon to Shinji (and was obviously using it as a substitute for "you humans"). He could not have been a more obvious Angel (to the viewers, at least) if he'd had it tattooed on his forehead. (Probably the only reason he wasn't found out sooner was, A, the short amount of time he was there, and B, that he talked in so much metaphor as to be indecipherable at times.) This also gave him a sort of image of purity. Naivete, maybe. His profession of love to Shinji added to that. Of course, the 'twist' that he would turn out to be an Angel was obvious. However, when he saw Adam and realized it was Lilith, the point of it all suddenly made sense. Earlier he had wondered why humans would use things abhorrent to them like the Evas to survive. Now he understood: because humans want to live. Remember, to him life and death are of equal value. So he figured, if humans want to live that badly, he should be the one to die so that they could live. At the end of the episode, Shinji believes that Kaworu should have been the one to live, because of that purity. So the purpose of Adam turning out to be Lilith was to highlight that, as Misato says, humans deserve to live because they have the will to live. ...Of course, none of this relates to what purpose the reveal served in terms of the whole series. Within the series, all it seems to do is shatter everything we thought we knew about what was going on, adding a whole new layer of questions and mysteries. Probably that was, among other things, so that we would be as confused going into EoE as Shinji was.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Thu Jun 17, 2010 5:59 pm I've always thought it was a mistake from them to call Lilith Adam. It's confusing as hell and it meaningless for the audience. In rebuild the fact that they clearly state it's Lilith from the beginning makes it feel like they kinda admit it was a mistake. (if it is indeed Lilith and not something else, the moon giant could be Lilith as well...)

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jun 17, 2010 6:02 pm Jinroh wrote: I've always thought it was a mistake from them to call Lilith Adam. It's confusing as hell and it meaningless for the audience. In rebuild the fact that they clearly state it's Lilith from the beginning makes it feel like they kinda admit it was a mistake. (if it is indeed Lilith and not something else, the moon giant could be Lilith as well...)

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jun 17, 2010 6:12 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: It was directed at essel. I get the impression often that there is a 'we know enough, stop digging people' attitude coming there that can be nasty and harsh at times, in example how he was starting to respond to you in my opinion. I hope it makes sense now; hope I'm not making another tangent, it was supposed to be clear the first time.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 17, 2010 6:16 pm I never said Anno had it planned out from the START. Myself and a couple of others here generally agree that Kaji's revelation to Misato was probably supposed to be genuine at the time. However, since the Second Angel had been missing FROM THE BEGINNING (an absence NOT mirrored in the Proposal), I don't think it's unreasonable to suppose that some conception of Lilith always existed and that Anno decided that it was in the basement at some point BEFORE this script was made.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jun 17, 2010 6:21 pm I just find it bizarre to suggest that Anno had one of his subordinates draft up a script for an episode whose plot relies on Lilith being Lilith, without telling him about it. Even moreso with all the rushing around that was being done towards the end of production, why would he decide to waste everyone's time like that?
summary: []: [SPLIT] Lilith instead of Adam: Personal thoughts/debate.             Quote Post by Lucretius » Thu Jun 17, 2010 3:52 pm Split from this thread . ~ SSD ________ There's still absolutely no reason for the identity of Lilith to be a production secret, from episode 15 onwards or otherwise. It's a detail that no one would care about except egf posters, certainly nowhere near as significant as Rei's being an artificial human &#40
